name: Bounds2d
summary: The bounds2d class represents an axis aligned bounding box represented by two Point2d objects, upper left and lower right positions. The units utilized in the creation and modification Bounds2d are inches.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new Geom::Bounds2d.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: height
methods.summary: The #height method returns the height of the Geom::Bounds2d.
methods.remarks: The #height method returns the height of the Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: lower_right
methods.summary: The #lower_right method returns the Point2d of the lower right corner of the Geom::Bounds2d.
methods.remarks: The #lower_right method returns the Point2d of the lower right corner of the Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: set!
methods.signature: set!(other_bounds) => Geom::Bounds2d | set!(upper_left, lower_right) => Geom::Bounds2d | set!(x, y, width, height) => Geom::Bounds2d | set!(point_array) => Geom::Bounds2d | set!(float_array) => Geom::Bounds2d
methods.summary: The #set! method is used to update the dimensions and position of a Geom::Bounds2d object so that it matches the specified bounds.
methods.remarks: The #set! method is used to update the dimensions and position of a Geom::Bounds2d object so that it matches the specified bounds. The argument is anything that can be converted into a Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method returns an array which contains the Point2ds that define the Geom::Bounds2d.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method returns an array which contains the Point2ds that define the Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: upper_left
methods.summary: The #upper_left method returns the Point2d of the upper left corner of the Geom::Bounds2d.
methods.remarks: The #upper_left method returns the Point2d of the upper left corner of the Geom::Bounds2d.

methods.name: width
methods.summary: The #width method returns the width of the Geom::Bounds2d.
methods.remarks: The #width method returns the width of the Geom::Bounds2d.

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Geom::Bounds2ds are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Geom::Bounds2ds are equal. This checks whether the point values are the same
